  ┌─ WHAT YOU STILL NEED ─────────────────────────────────────
  │  1. │     DSR below threshold — more live track record is the
  │     only fix. Every month of live returns consistent with
  │     backtest adds significant weight.
  │
  │  2. │     Walk-forward instability — high Sharpe variance across
  │     regimes suggests the edge may be period-specific.
  │     Investigate which folds underperformed and why.
  │
  │  3. │     Sharpe below 2.5 — acceptable but an allocator will
  │     ask what is suppressing it. Ensure slippage
  │     assumptions are realistic.
  │
  │  4. │     Calmar below 3.0 — return per unit of MaxDD is below
  │     institutional benchmark. Either CAGR needs to improve
  │     or MaxDD needs to tighten.
  │
  │  5. │     Sortino below 4.0 — more downside volatility than
  │     expected for this Sharpe. Check whether losses are
  │     clustering or spreading uniformly.
  │
  │  6. │     Regime robustness — Sharpe decay or CAGR ratio
  │     suggests performance deteriorated in the second half
  │     of the dataset. Characterise what changed and whether
  │     it is structural.
  │
  │  7. │     Slippage resilience — Sharpe degrades materially at 2×
  │     base slippage. Live execution costs may be higher than
  │     assumed. Stress-test against wider spreads.
  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  ┌─ WHAT YOU HAVE — DUE DILIGENCE CHECKLIST ─────────────────
  │  ✅ DSR and MTL — statistical validity proven (94.03% DSR)
  │  ✅ MC reshuffle (2,000 iterations) and block bootstrap stress tests
  │  ✅ Neighbor plateau test — joint parameter robustness confirmed
  │  ✅ Slippage, cost curve, and capacity curve — operational concerns addressed
  │  ✅ Shock injection — tail risk quantified under artificial stress scenarios
  │  ✅ Drawdown episode analysis — depth, duration, and recovery tracked
  │  ✅ Regime robustness — first-half vs second-half performance split
  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  ┌─ WHAT YOU STILL NEED ─────────────────────────────────────
  │  1. │     DSR below threshold — more live track record is the
  │     only fix. Every month of live returns consistent with
  │     backtest adds significant weight.
  │
  │  2. │     Walk-forward instability — high Sharpe variance across
  │     regimes suggests the edge may be period-specific.
  │     Investigate which folds underperformed and why.
  │
  │  3. │     Sharpe below 2.5 — acceptable but an allocator will
  │     ask what is suppressing it. Ensure slippage
  │     assumptions are realistic.
  │
  │  4. │     Sortino below 4.0 — more downside volatility than
  │     expected for this Sharpe. Check whether losses are
  │     clustering or spreading uniformly.
  │
  │  5. │     Regime robustness — Sharpe decay or CAGR ratio
  │     suggests performance deteriorated in the second half
  │     of the dataset. Characterise what changed and whether
  │     it is structural.
  │
  │  6. │     Slippage resilience — Sharpe degrades materially at 2×
  │     base slippage. Live execution costs may be higher than
  │     assumed. Stress-test against wider spreads.
  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  ┌─ BOTTOM LINE ──────────────────────────────────────────────

  │  failures and 3 marginal results that require attention
  │  before this is deployable. Failing metrics: Walk-Forward
  │  Stability (CV), Regime Robustness. Focus on the gating
  │  metrics first — Sharpe and DSR must both clear before
  │  the other scores matter.

  │     Goal: Sharpe≥1.8 at 2× slippage  (≥2.5 ideal)   Actual: 0.808
  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  ┌─ WHAT YOU HAVE — DUE DILIGENCE CHECKLIST ─────────────────
  │  ✅ DSR and MTL — statistical validity proven (50.87% DSR)
  │  ✅ MC reshuffle (2,000 iterations) and block bootstrap stress tests
  │  ✅ Neighbor plateau test — joint parameter robustness confirmed
  │  ✅ Slippage, cost curve, and capacity curve — operational concerns addressed
  │  ✅ Shock injection — tail risk quantified under artificial stress scenarios
  │  ✅ Drawdown episode analysis — depth, duration, and recovery tracked
  │  ✅ Regime robustness — first-half vs second-half performance split
  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  ┌─ WHAT YOU STILL NEED ─────────────────────────────────────
  │  1. │     DSR below threshold — more live track record is the
  │     only fix. Every month of live returns consistent with
  │     backtest adds significant weight.
  │
  │  2. │     Walk-forward instability — high Sharpe variance across
  │     regimes suggests the edge may be period-specific.
  │     Investigate which folds underperformed and why.
  │
  │  3. │     Sharpe below 2.5 — acceptable but an allocator will
  │     ask what is suppressing it. Ensure slippage
  │     assumptions are realistic.
  │
  │  4. │     Calmar below 3.0 — return per unit of MaxDD is below
  │     institutional benchmark. Either CAGR needs to improve
  │     or MaxDD needs to tighten.
  │
  │  5. │     Sortino below 4.0 — more downside volatility than
  │     expected for this Sharpe. Check whether losses are
  │     clustering or spreading uniformly.
  │
  │  6. │     Regime robustness — Sharpe decay or CAGR ratio
  │     suggests performance deteriorated in the second half
  │     of the dataset. Characterise what changed and whether
  │     it is structural.
  │
  │  7. │     Slippage resilience — Sharpe degrades materially at 2×
  │     base slippage. Live execution costs may be higher than
  │     assumed. Stress-test against wider spreads.
  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  ┌─ BOTTOM LINE ──────────────────────────────────────────────
  │  The strategy scores 31/100. There are 7 outright
  │  failures and 0 marginal results that require attention
  │  before this is deployable. Failing metrics: Deflated
  │  Sharpe Ratio (DSR), Walk-Forward Stability (CV), Sharpe
  │  Ratio, Calmar Ratio, Sortino Ratio, Regime Robustness,
  │  Slippage Resilience. Focus on the gating metrics first —
  │  Sharpe and DSR must both clear before the other scores
  │  matter.
  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  │  ✅ Drawdown episode analysis — depth, duration, and recovery tracked

  ┌─ WHAT YOU STILL NEED ─────────────────────────────────────
  │  1. │     DSR below threshold — more live track record is the
  │     only fix. Every month of live returns consistent with
  │     backtest adds significant weight.
  │
  │  2. │     Walk-forward instability — high Sharpe variance across
  │     regimes suggests the edge may be period-specific.
  │     Investigate which folds underperformed and why.
  │
  │  3. │     Sharpe below 2.5 — acceptable but an allocator will
  │     ask what is suppressing it. Ensure slippage
  │     assumptions are realistic.
  │
  │  4. │     Calmar below 3.0 — return per unit of MaxDD is below
  │     institutional benchmark. Either CAGR needs to improve
  │     or MaxDD needs to tighten.
  │
  │  5. │     Sortino below 4.0 — more downside volatility than
  │     expected for this Sharpe. Check whether losses are
  │     clustering or spreading uniformly.
  │
  │  6. │     Regime robustness — Sharpe decay or CAGR ratio
  │     suggests performance deteriorated in the second half
  │     of the dataset. Characterise what changed and whether
  │     it is structural.
  │
  │  7. │     Slippage resilience — Sharpe degrades materially at 2×
  │     base slippage. Live execution costs may be higher than
  │     assumed. Stress-test against wider spreads.
  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  ┌─ BOTTOM LINE ──────────────────────────────────────────────

  │  before this is deployable. Failing metrics: Walk-Forward

  ┌─ SUPPORTING METRICS  (16/23)  Grade: B− ──────────────────
  │
  │  ✅ Neighbor Plateau (Parameter Robustness)  —  8/8
  │  97.5% of neighbors within 0.5 Sharpe — parameters sit on
  │  a broad stable plateau. Operational imprecision is
  │  tolerable.
  │     Goal: ≥70%  (≥80% ideal)   Actual: 97.5%
  │
  │  ✅ MC Reshuffle % Losing  —  8/8
  │  0.00% of 2,000 reshuffles lost money — positive
  │  expectation is robust to path ordering.
  │     Goal: <1%  (<0.5% ideal)   Actual: 0.00%
  │
  │  ❌ Slippage Resilience  —  0/7
  │  Sharpe 1.491 at 2× slippage — fragile to cost increases.
  │  Backtest slippage assumptions may be optimistic.
  │     Goal: Sharpe≥1.8 at 2× slippage  (≥2.5 ideal)   Actual: 1.491
  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  ┌─ WHAT YOU HAVE — DUE DILIGENCE CHECKLIST ─────────────────
  │  ✅ DSR and MTL — statistical validity proven (77.52% DSR)
  │  ✅ MC reshuffle (2,000 iterations) and block bootstrap stress tests
  │  ✅ Neighbor plateau test — joint parameter robustness confirmed
  │  ✅ Slippage, cost curve, and capacity curve — operational concerns addressed
  │  ✅ Shock injection — tail risk quantified under artificial stress scenarios
  │  ✅ Drawdown episode analysis — depth, duration, and recovery tracked
  │  ✅ Regime robustness — first-half vs second-half performance split
  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  ┌─ WHAT YOU STILL NEED ─────────────────────────────────────
  │  1. │     DSR below threshold — more live track record is the
  │     only fix. Every month of live returns consistent with
  │     backtest adds significant weight.
  │
  │  2. │     Walk-forward instability — high Sharpe variance across
  │     regimes suggests the edge may be period-specific.
  │     Investigate which folds underperformed and why.
  │
  │  3. │     Sharpe below 2.5 — acceptable but an allocator will
  │     ask what is suppressing it. Ensure slippage
  │     assumptions are realistic.
  │
  │  4. │     Calmar below 3.0 — return per unit of MaxDD is below
  │     institutional benchmark. Either CAGR needs to improve
  │     or MaxDD needs to tighten.
  │
  │  5. │     Sortino below 4.0 — more downside volatility than
  │     expected for this Sharpe. Check whether losses are
  │     clustering or spreading uniformly.
  │
  │  6. │     Regime robustness — Sharpe decay or CAGR ratio
  │     suggests performance deteriorated in the second half
  │     of the dataset. Characterise what changed and whether
  │     it is structural.
  │
  │  7. │     Slippage resilience — Sharpe degrades materially at 2×
  │     base slippage. Live execution costs may be higher than
  │     assumed. Stress-test against wider spreads.
  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  ┌─ BOTTOM LINE ──────────────────────────────────────────────
  │  The strategy scores 36/100. There are 4 outright
  │  failures and 3 marginal results that require attention
  │  before this is deployable. Failing metrics: Walk-Forward
  │  Stability (CV), Sortino Ratio, Regime Robustness,
  │  Slippage Resilience. Focus on the gating metrics first —
  │  Sharpe and DSR must both clear before the other scores
  │  matter.
  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  ┌─ WHAT YOU STILL NEED ─────────────────────────────────────
  │  1. │     DSR below threshold — more live track record is the
  │     only fix. Every month of live returns consistent with
  │     backtest adds significant weight.
  │
  │  2. │     Walk-forward instability — high Sharpe variance across
  │     regimes suggests the edge may be period-specific.
  │     Investigate which folds underperformed and why.
  │
  │  3. │     Sharpe below 2.5 — acceptable but an allocator will
  │     ask what is suppressing it. Ensure slippage
  │     assumptions are realistic.
  │
  │  4. │     Calmar below 3.0 — return per unit of MaxDD is below
  │     institutional benchmark. Either CAGR needs to improve
  │     or MaxDD needs to tighten.
  │
  │  5. │     Sortino below 4.0 — more downside volatility than
  │     expected for this Sharpe. Check whether losses are
  │     clustering or spreading uniformly.
  │
  │  6. │     Regime robustness — Sharpe decay or CAGR ratio
  │     suggests performance deteriorated in the second half
  │     of the dataset. Characterise what changed and whether
  │     it is structural.
  │
  │  7. │     Slippage resilience — Sharpe degrades materially at 2×
  │     base slippage. Live execution costs may be higher than
  │     assumed. Stress-test against wider spreads.
  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  ┌─ WHAT YOU HAVE — DUE DILIGENCE CHECKLIST ─────────────────
  │  ✅ DSR and MTL — statistical validity proven (67.78% DSR)
  │  ✅ MC reshuffle (2,000 iterations) and block bootstrap stress tests
  │  ✅ Neighbor plateau test — joint parameter robustness confirmed
  │  ✅ Slippage, cost curve, and capacity curve — operational concerns addressed
  │  ✅ Shock injection — tail risk quantified under artificial stress scenarios
  │  ✅ Drawdown episode analysis — depth, duration, and recovery tracked
  │  ✅ Regime robustness — first-half vs second-half performance split
  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  ┌─ WHAT YOU STILL NEED ─────────────────────────────────────
  │  1. │     DSR below threshold — more live track record is the
  │     only fix. Every month of live returns consistent with
  │     backtest adds significant weight.
  │
  │  2. │     Walk-forward instability — high Sharpe variance across
  │     regimes suggests the edge may be period-specific.
  │     Investigate which folds underperformed and why.
  │
  │  3. │     Sharpe below 2.5 — acceptable but an allocator will
  │     ask what is suppressing it. Ensure slippage
  │     assumptions are realistic.
  │
  │  4. │     Calmar below 3.0 — return per unit of MaxDD is below
  │     institutional benchmark. Either CAGR needs to improve
  │     or MaxDD needs to tighten.
  │
  │  5. │     Sortino below 4.0 — more downside volatility than
  │     expected for this Sharpe. Check whether losses are
  │     clustering or spreading uniformly.
  │
  │  6. │     Regime robustness — Sharpe decay or CAGR ratio
  │     suggests performance deteriorated in the second half
  │     of the dataset. Characterise what changed and whether
  │     it is structural.
  │
  │  7. │     Slippage resilience — Sharpe degrades materially at 2×
  │     base slippage. Live execution costs may be higher than
  │     assumed. Stress-test against wider spreads.
  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  ┌─ BOTTOM LINE ──────────────────────────────────────────────
  │  The strategy scores 32/100. There are 6 outright
  │  failures and 1 marginal results that require attention
  │  before this is deployable. Failing metrics: Deflated
  │  Sharpe Ratio (DSR), Walk-Forward Stability (CV), Sharpe
  │  Ratio, Sortino Ratio, Regime Robustness, Slippage
  │  Resilience. Focus on the gating metrics first — Sharpe

  │     only fix. Every month of live returns consistent with
  │     backtest adds significant weight.
  │
  │  2. │     Walk-forward instability — high Sharpe variance across
  │     regimes suggests the edge may be period-specific.
  │     Investigate which folds underperformed and why.
  │
  │  3. │     Sharpe below 2.5 — acceptable but an allocator will
  │     ask what is suppressing it. Ensure slippage
  │     assumptions are realistic.
  │
  │  4. │     Calmar below 3.0 — return per unit of MaxDD is below
  │     institutional benchmark. Either CAGR needs to improve
  │     or MaxDD needs to tighten.
  │
  │  5. │     Sortino below 4.0 — more downside volatility than
  │     expected for this Sharpe. Check whether losses are
  │     clustering or spreading uniformly.
  │
  │  6. │     Regime robustness — Sharpe decay or CAGR ratio
  │     suggests performance deteriorated in the second half
  │     of the dataset. Characterise what changed and whether
  │     it is structural.
  │
  │  7. │     MC % losing above 2% — more path-ordering sensitivity
  │     than ideal. The positive expectation may be partially
  │     sequence-dependent.
  │
  │  8. │     Slippage resilience — Sharpe degrades materially at 2×
  │     base slippage. Live execution costs may be higher than
  │     assumed. Stress-test against wider spreads.

  │     regimes suggests the edge may be period-specific.

  │     or MaxDD needs to tighten.

  │     clustering or spreading uniformly.
  │
  │  6. │     Regime robustness — Sharpe decay or CAGR ratio
  │     suggests performance deteriorated in the second half
  │     of the dataset. Characterise what changed and whether
  │     it is structural.
  │
  │  7. │     Slippage resilience — Sharpe degrades materially at 2×

  STEP 6 — PBO  (Probability of Backtest Overfitting)
